#include "array.h"
#include "class.h"

#define KNI_ReturnBoolean(value)   return (value) ? 1 : 0
#define KNI_ReturnByte(value)   return (jbyte) (value)
#define KNI_ReturnChar(value)   return (jchar) (value)
#define KNI_ReturnShort(value)   return (jshort) (value)

Detailed Description

CLDC native interface

Definition in file kni.h.

Define Documentation

#define JELATINE_KNI_H   (1)

kni.h inclusion macro

Definition at line 28 of file kni.h.

#define KNI_DeclareHandle (   handle  )     jobject handle = _KNI_push_handle(&__handles__[__declared_count__++])

Declares a handle that can be used as an input, output, or in/out parameter to an appropriate KNI function. The initial value of the handle is unspecified. If the programmer has not allocated enough space for the handle in the preceding call to KNI_StartHandles, the behavior of this function is unspecified

Parameters:
handle The name by which the handle will be identified between the calls to KNI_StartHandles and KNI_EndHandles / KNI_EndHandlesAndReturnObject

Definition at line 1481 of file kni.h.

Referenced by java_lang_Class_forName(), java_lang_Class_getInternalName(), java_lang_Class_isAssignableFrom(), java_lang_Class_isInstance(), java_lang_Class_newInstance(), java_lang_Object__wait(), java_lang_Object_getClass(), java_lang_Object_notify(), java_lang_Object_notifyAll(), java_lang_ref_WeakReference_addToWeakReferenceList(), java_lang_String_intern(), java_lang_System_arraycopy(), java_lang_System_identityHashCode(), java_lang_Thread_currentThread(), java_lang_Thread_interrupt(), java_lang_Thread_join(), and java_lang_Thread_start().

#define KNI_EINVAL   (-6)

Failure due to incorrect arguments

Definition at line 181 of file kni.h.

#define KNI_EndHandles (  ) 
Value:
while (__declared_count__--) { \
        thread_pop_root(); \
    } \
}

Undeclares and unallocates the handles defined after the preceding KNI_StartHandles() call

Definition at line 1516 of file kni.h.

Referenced by java_lang_Class_isAssignableFrom(), java_lang_Class_isInstance(), java_lang_Object__wait(), java_lang_Object_notify(), java_lang_Object_notifyAll(), java_lang_ref_WeakReference_addToWeakReferenceList(), java_lang_System_arraycopy(), java_lang_System_identityHashCode(), java_lang_Thread_interrupt(), java_lang_Thread_join(), and java_lang_Thread_start().

#define KNI_EndHandlesAndReturnObject (   objectHandle  ) 
Value:
while (__declared_count__--) { \
        thread_pop_root(); \
    } \
    return (uintptr_t) *objectHandle; \
}

Undeclares and unallocates the handles defined after the preceding KNI_StartHandles call. At the same time, this function will terminate the execution of the native function immediately and return an object reference back to the caller

Parameters:
objectHandle The handle from which the result value will be read

Definition at line 1528 of file kni.h.

Referenced by java_lang_Class_forName(), java_lang_Class_getInternalName(), java_lang_Class_newInstance(), java_lang_Object_getClass(), java_lang_String_intern(), and java_lang_Thread_currentThread().

#define KNI_ReturnBoolean (   value  )     return (value) ? 1 : 0

Returns a boolean value from a native function. Calling the function will terminate the execution of the native function immediately

Parameters:
value The result value of the native method

#define KNI_StartHandles (   n  ) 
Value:
{ \
    size_t __declared_count__ = 0; \
    uintptr_t __handles__[n]

Allocates enough space for n handles for the current native method

n A positive integer specifying the number of handles to allocate

Function Documentation

static jobject _KNI_push_handle ( jobject  handle  )  [inline, static]

Internal function used for recording the new handles as temporary roots while declaring them. This function also clears the passed handle so that the garbage collector will not see a garbage reference

Parameters:
handle A KNI object handle
Returns:
The handle argument

Definition at line 1457 of file kni.h.

References thread_push_root().

void KNI_FatalError ( const char *  message  ) 

Prints an error message to the system’s standard error output, and terminates the execution of the virtual machine immediately

Parameters:
message The error message as a UTF-8 string

Definition at line 77 of file kni.c.

References vm_fail().

static void KNI_FindClass ( const char *  name,
jclass  classHandle 
) [inline, static]

Initializes a handle with a reference to the named class or interface. The function assumes that the class has already been loaded to the system and properly initialized. If this is not the case, or if no class is found, the handle will be initialized to NULL

Parameters:
name The descriptor of the class or interface to be returned. The name is represented as a UTF-8 string
classHandle A handle in which the class pointer will be returned

Definition at line 210 of file kni.h.

References bcl_find_class(), and class_get_object().

static jsize KNI_GetArrayLength ( jarray  array  )  [inline, static]

Returns the number of elements in a given Java array object. The array argument may denote an array of any element type, including primitive types or reference types. If the given array handle is NULL, the function returns -1

Parameters:
array A handle pointing to an array object whose length is to be determined
Returns:
Returns the number of elements in the array, or -1 if the given array handle is NULL

Definition at line 878 of file kni.h.

static jboolean KNI_GetBooleanArrayElement ( jbooleanArray  arrayHandle,
jsize  index 
) [inline, static]

Returns an element of an array of booleans. The given array reference must not be NULL. No array type checking or index range checking is performed

Parameters:
arrayHandle A handle pointing to an array object
index The index of the element in the array. Index 0 denotes the first element in the array
Returns:
Returns the element at position index of an array of booleans

Definition at line 894 of file kni.h.

References array_get_data().

static jboolean KNI_GetBooleanField ( jobject  objectHandle,
jfieldID  fieldID 
) [inline, static]

Returns the value of an instance field of type boolean. The field to access is denoted by fieldID

Parameters:
objectHandle A handle pointing to an object whose field is to be accessed
fieldID The field ID of the given instance field
Returns:
The value of the specified instance field

Definition at line 322 of file kni.h.

static jbyte KNI_GetByteArrayElement ( jbyteArray  arrayHandle,
jsize  index 
) [inline, static]

Returns an element of an array of bytes. The given array reference must not be NULL. No array type checking or index range checking is performed

Parameters:
arrayHandle A handle pointing to an array object
index The index of the element in the array. Index 0 denotes the first element in the array
Returns:
Returns the element at position index of an array of bytes

References array_get_data().

Returns:
The value of the specified instance field

Definition at line 349 of file kni.h.

static void KNI_GetClassPointer ( jclass  toHandle  )  [inline, static]

Reads the value of the class pointer in the current stack frame of a static native method, and stores the value in the handle toHandle

Parameters:
toHandle A handle to contain the class pointer

Definition at line 1385 of file kni.h.

References class_get_object(), and thread_self().

jfieldID KNI_GetFieldID ( jclass  classHandle,
const char *  name,
const char *  signature 
)

Returns the field ID of an instance field of the class represented by classHandle. The field is specified by its name and signature. The Get<Type>Field and Set<Type>Field families of accessor functions use field IDs to retrieve the value of instance fields. The field must be accessible from the class referred to by classHandle

Parameters:
classHandle A handle pointing to a class whose field ID will be retrieved
name The field name as a UTF-8 string
signature The field descriptor as a UTF-8 string
Returns:
Returns a field ID or NULL if the lookup fails for any reason

Definition at line 98 of file kni.c.

References bcl_get_class_by_id(), class_get_field(), field_is_static(), JAVA_LANG_CLASS_REF2PTR, and field_t::offset.

static jint KNI_GetIntArrayElement ( jintArray  arrayHandle,
jsize  index 
) [inline, static]

Returns an element of an array of ints. The given array reference must not be NULL. No array type checking or index range checking is performed

Parameters:
arrayHandle A handle pointing to an array object
index The index of the element in the array. Index 0 denotes the first element in the array
Returns:
Returns the element at position index of an array of ints

References array_get_data().

static void KNI_GetObjectArrayElement ( jobjectArray  arrayHandle,
jint  index,
jobject  toHandle 
) [inline, static]

Returns an element of an array of a reference type. The given array handle must not be NULL. No array type checking or index range checking is performed

Parameters:
arrayHandle A handle pointing to an array object
index The index of the element in the array. Index 0 denotes the first element in the array
toHandle A handle to which the return value will be assigned

jclass  classHandle 
) [inline, static]

Sets the handle classHandle to point to the class of the object represented by objectHandle

Parameters:
objectHandle A handle pointing to an object whose class is being sought
classHandle A handle which, upon return of this function, will contain a reference to the class of this object

Definition at line 278 of file kni.h.

References class_get_object(), and header_get_class().

static void KNI_GetObjectField ( jobject  objectHandle,
jfieldID  fieldID,
jobject  toHandle 
) [inline, static]

Returns the value of a field of a reference type, and assigns it to the handle toHandle. The field to access is denoted by fieldID

Parameters:
objectHandle A handle pointing to an object whose whose field is to be accessed
fieldID The field ID of the given instance field
toHandle A handle to which the return value will be assigned

Definition at line 425 of file kni.h.

static jboolean KNI_GetParameterAsBoolean ( jint  index  )  [inline, static]

Returns the value of a parameter of type boolean at physical location specified by index. Parameter indices are mapped from left to right. Index value 1 refers to the leftmost parameter that has been passed on to the native method. Remember that parameters of type long or double take up two entries on the operand stack. No type checking or index range checking is performed.

Parameters:
index The index of a parameter of a primitive type to the native method. Index value 1 refers to the leftmost parameter in the Java method.
Returns:
Returns the argument of a primitive type at position index in the list of arguments to the native method.

Definition at line 1236 of file kni.h.

References thread_t::fp, stack_frame_t::locals, and thread_self().

static jbyte KNI_GetParameterAsByte ( jint  index  )  [inline, static]

Returns the value of a parameter of type byte at physical location specified by index. Parameter indices are mapped from left to right. Index value 1 refers to the leftmost parameter that has been passed on to the native method. Remember that parameters of type long or double take up two entries on the operand stack. No type checking or index range checking is performed.

Parameters:
index The index of a parameter of a primitive type to the native method. Index value 1 refers to the leftmost parameter in the Java method.
Returns:
Returns the argument of a primitive type at position index in the list of arguments to the native method.

Definition at line 1252 of file kni.h.

References thread_t::fp, stack_frame_t::locals, and thread_self().

Referenced by jelatine_VMOutputStream_write_to_stderr(), and jelatine_VMOutputStream_write_to_stdout().

Index value 1 refers to the leftmost parameter that has been passed on to the native method. Remember that parameters of type long or double take up two entries on the operand stack. No type checking or index range checking is performed.

Parameters:
index The index of a parameter of a primitive type to the native method. Index value 1 refers to the leftmost parameter in the Java method.
Returns:
Returns the argument of a primitive type at position index in the list of arguments to the native method.

index The index of a parameter of a primitive type to the native method. Index value 1 refers to the leftmost parameter in the Java method.

static void KNI_GetParameterAsObject ( jint  index,
jobject  toHandle 
) [inline, static]

Reads the value (object reference) of the parameter specified by index, and stores it in the handle toHandle. Parameter indices are mapped from left to right. Index value 1 refers to the leftmost parameter that has been passed on to the native method. No type checking or index range checking is performed

Parameters:
index The index of a parameter of a reference type to the native method. Index value 1 refers to the leftmost parameter in the Java method
toHandle A handle to to which the return value will be assigned

static jshort KNI_GetParameterAsShort ( jint  index  )  [inline, static]

Returns the value of a parameter of type short at physical location specified by index. Parameter indices are mapped from left to right. Index value 1 refers to the leftmost parameter that has been passed on to the native method. Remember that parameters of type long or double take up two entries on the operand stack. No type checking or index range checking is performed.

Parameters:
index The index of a parameter of a primitive type to the native method. Index value 1 refers to the leftmost parameter in the Java method.
Returns:
Returns the argument of a primitive type at position index in the list of arguments to the native method.

static void KNI_GetRawArrayRegion ( jarray  arrayHandle,
jsize  offset,
jsize  n,
jbyte *  dstBuffer 
) [inline, static]

Gets a region of n bytes of an array of a primitive type. The given array handle must not be NULL. No array type checking or range checking is performed

Parameters:
arrayHandle A handle initialized with the array reference
offset A byte offset within the array
n The number of bytes of raw data to get
dstBuffer The destination of the data as bytes

References array_get_data().

static jshort KNI_GetShortArrayElement ( jshortArray  arrayHandle,
jsize  index 
) [inline, static]

Returns an element of an array of shorts. The given array reference must not be NULL. No array type checking or index range checking is performed

Parameters:
arrayHandle A handle pointing to an array object
index The index of the element in the array. Index 0 denotes the first element in the array
Returns:
Returns the element at position index of an array of shorts

References array_get_data().

objectHandle A handle pointing to an object whose field is to be accessed
fieldID The field ID of the given instance field

Returns:
Returns the value of the specified static field

Definition at line 597 of file kni.h.

jfieldID KNI_GetStaticFieldID ( jclass  classHandle,
const char *  name,
const char *  signature 
)

Returns the field ID of a static field of the class represented by classHandle. The field is specified by its name and signature. The GetStatic<Type>Field and SetStatic<Type>Field families of accessor functions use field IDs to retrieve the value of static fields. The field must be accessible from the class referred to by classHandle

Parameters:
classHandle A handle pointing to a class whose field ID will be retrieved
name The field name as a UTF-8 string
signature The field descriptor as a UTF-8 string
Returns:
Returns a field ID or NULL if the lookup fails for any reason

Definition at line 129 of file kni.c.

References bcl_get_class_by_id(), class_get_field(), field_is_static(), JAVA_LANG_CLASS_REF2PTR, field_t::offset, class_t::static_data, and static_field_data_ptr().

jfieldID  fieldID 
) [inline, static]

Returns the value of a static field of int type. The field to access is denoted by fieldID.

Parameters:
classHandle A handle pointing to a class or interface whose static field is to be accessed
fieldID A field ID of the given class
Returns:
Returns the value of the specified static field

static jsize KNI_GetStringLength ( jstring  stringHandle  )  [inline, static]

Returns the number of Unicode characters in a java.lang.String object. If the given string handle is NULL, the function returns -1

Parameters:
stringHandle A handle pointing to a java.lang.String string object whose length is to be determined
Returns:
The length of the string, or -1 if the given string handle is NULL

Definition at line 819 of file kni.h.

References JAVA_LANG_STRING_REF2PTR.

void KNI_GetStringRegion ( jstring  stringHandle,
jsize  offset,
jsize  n,
jchar *  jcharbuf 
)

Copies a number of Unicode characters from a java.lang.String object (denoted by stringHandle), beginning at offset, to the given buffer jcharbuf. No range checking is performed. It is the responsibility of the native function programmer to allocate the return buffer, and to make sure that it is large enough

Parameters:
stringHandle A handle pointing to a java.lang.String object whose contents are to be copied
offset The offset within the string object at which to start copying (offset 0: first character in the Java string.)
n The number of 16-bit Unicode characters to copy
jcharbuf A pointer to a buffer to hold the Unicode characters

Definition at line 164 of file kni.c.

References array_get_data(), JAVA_LANG_STRING_REF2PTR, and java_lang_String_t::value.

static void KNI_GetSuperClass ( jclass  classHandle,
jclass  superClassHandle 
) [inline, static]

Initializes the parent handle to contain a pointer to the superclass of the given class (represented by cl). If cl represents the class java.lang.Object, then the function sets the parent handle to NULL

Parameters:
classHandle A handle initialized with a reference to the class object whose superclass is to be determined
superClassHandle A handle which, upon return from this function, will contain a reference to the superclass object

Definition at line 229 of file kni.h.

References bcl_get_class_by_id(), class_get_object(), class_get_parent(), JAVA_LANG_CLASS_REF2PTR, and class_t::parent.

static void KNI_GetThisPointer ( jobject  toHandle  )  [inline, static]

Reads the value of the 'this' pointer in the current stack frame of an instance (non-static) native method, and stores the value in the handle toHandle

Parameters:
toHandle A handle to contain the 'this' pointer

static jint KNI_GetVersion ( void   )  [inline, static]

The native interface version

Definition at line 193 of file kni.h.

static jboolean KNI_IsAssignableFrom ( jclass  cl1,
jclass  cl2 
) [inline, static]

Determines whether an object of class or interface cl1 can be safely cast to a class or interface cl2

Parameters:
cl1 Handle initialized with a reference to the first class or interface argument
cl2 Handle initialized with a reference to the second class or interface argument
Returns:
KNI_TRUE if any of the following is true: the first and second argument refer to the same class or interface, the first argument refers to a subclass of the second argument, the first argument refers to a class that has the second argument as one of its interfaces or the first and second arguments both refer to array of classes with element types X and Y, and KNI_IsAssignableFrom(X, Y) is KNI_TRUE. Otherwise KNI_FALSE

Definition at line 252 of file kni.h.

References bcl_get_class_by_id(), bcl_is_assignable(), JAVA_LANG_CLASS_REF2PTR, and KNI_TRUE.

Referenced by java_lang_Class_isAssignableFrom().

static jboolean KNI_IsInstanceOf ( jobject  objectHandle,
jclass  classHandle 
) [inline, static]

Tests whether an object represented by objectHandle is an instance of a class or interface represented by classHandle

Parameters:
objectHandle A handle pointing to an object
classHandle A handle pointing to a class or interface
Returns:
Returns KNI_TRUE if the given object is an instance of given class, KNI_FALSE otherwise

jobject  obj2 
) [inline, static]

obj1 A handle pointing to an object
obj2 A handle pointing to an object
Returns:
KNI_TRUE if the handles refer to the same object, KNI_FALSE otherwise

jsize  length,
jobject  stringHandle 
) [inline, static]

Creates a java.lang.String object from the given Unicode sequence. The resulting object is returned to the caller via the stringHandle handle

uchars A Unicode sequence that will make up the contents of the new string object
length The length of the string
stringHandle A handle to hold the reference to the new java.lang.String object

jshort  value 
) [inline, static]

Sets the value of a static field of short type. The field to modify is denoted by fieldID

Parameters:
classHandle A handle pointing to the class or interface whose static field is to be modified
fieldID A field ID of the given class
value The value to set to the static field

Definition at line 731 of file kni.h.

jint KNI_ThrowNew ( const char *  name,
const char *  message 
)

Instantiates an exception object with the message specified by message, and causes that exception to be thrown. A thrown exception will be pending in the current thread, but does not immediately disrupt native code execution. The actual exception will be thrown when the program control returns from the native function back to the virtual machine. Note that KNI_ThrowNew does not run the constructor on the new exception object.

Parameters:
name The name of a java.lang.Throwable class as a UTF-8 string
message The message used to construct the java.lang.Throwable object; represented as a UTF-8 string
Returns:
KNI_OK on success; otherwise KNI_ERR

Definition at line 52 of file kni.c.

References bcl_resolve_class(), stack_frame_t::cl, java_lang_Throwable_t::detailMessage, thread_t::exception, thread_t::fp, gc_new(), JAVA_LANG_STRING_PTR2REF, JAVA_LANG_THROWABLE_REF2PTR, jstring_create_from_utf8(), and thread_self().

Referenced by arraycopy_ref(), java_lang_Class_forName(), java_lang_Class_isAssignableFrom(), java_lang_Object__wait(), java_lang_Object_notify(), java_lang_Object_notifyAll(), java_lang_System_arraycopy(), java_lang_Thread_sleep(), thread_join(), thread_sleep(), and thread_wait().
